pattern: A flood fill identifies the connected component, but only the cells that touch the grid edge or a differently-colored neighbor are borders, so mark them and recolor afterward to avoid corrupting the in-progress search.
hints: Explore the connected region of same-colored cells starting from the given cell using DFS or BFS.
A cell is on the border if it lies on the grid boundary or has at least one 4-directional neighbor that is NOT part of the component.
Decide which cells are borders first (or use a visited marker) before recoloring, otherwise recolored cells would falsely look like 'different color' neighbors during the traversal.

edge_inputs.description: tiny grid, component of two cells
interior cell of a larger component
center stays original; only the ring is bordered
recolor equals existing color of a neighbor must not merge components
component touches only part of the boundary

stress.description: random small grid with few colors so components are non-trivial
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    m = rng.randint(1, max(1, min(size, 12)))
    n = rng.randint(1, max(1, min(size, 12)))
    grid = [[rng.randint(1, 3) for _ in range(n)] for _ in range(m)]
    r = rng.randint(0, m - 1)
    c = rng.randint(0, n - 1)
    color = rng.randint(1, 5)
    return [grid, r, c, color]
stress.note: small color palette guarantees meaningful connected components
